input.issue_title: Blocked HTTP processing on malformed Host header
input.issue_body:
### Version

4.5.23

### Context

When running the following command on a minimal Quarkus/Vert.x app, sending a bad Host header like the following...

```
curl -H "Host: evil.com%3F" -v -s http://localhost:8080
```

... leads to an exception on the server side, and the connection is blocked as in the caller will never see a response on the TCP connection. See below for the exception. 

The expected outcome would have been a HTTP 400 response. 

This was discussed with the Vert.x maintainers in advance and was considered a regular bug, and not a security issue. 

This is the logged exception:

```
2026-01-19 14:39:14,504 traceId=54c3930ee18cbfcc576b4ef4441d2da6, parentId=, spanId=1bd1f47b7fa65ace, sampled=true ERROR [io.quarkus.vertx.core.runtime.VertxCoreRecorder] (vert.x-eventloop-thread-2) Uncaught exception received by Vert.x: java.lang.StringIndexOutOfBoundsException: Index 38 out of bounds for length 14
	at java.base/jdk.internal.util.Preconditions$1.apply(Preconditions.java:55)
	at java.base/jdk.internal.util.Preconditions$1.apply(Preconditions.java:52)
	at java.base/jdk.internal.util.Preconditions$4.apply(Preconditions.java:213)
	at java.base/jdk.internal.util.Preconditions$4.apply(Preconditions.java:210)
	at java.base/jdk.internal.util.Preconditions.outOfBounds(Preconditions.java:98)
	at java.base/jdk.internal.util.Preconditions.outOfBoundsCheckIndex(Preconditions.java:106)
	at java.base/jdk.internal.util.Preconditions.checkIndex(Preconditions.java:302)
	at java.base/java.lang.String.checkIndex(String.java:4832)
	at java.base/java.lang.StringLatin1.charAt(StringLatin1.java:46)
	at java.base/java.lang.String.charAt(String.java:1555)
	at io.vertx.core.net.impl.HostAndPortImpl.parseRegName(HostAndPortImpl.java:110)
	at io.vertx.core.net.impl.HostAndPortImpl.parseHost(HostAndPortImpl.java:25)
	at io.vertx.core.net.impl.HostAndPortImpl.isValidAuthority(HostAndPortImpl.java:154)
	at io.vertx.core.http.impl.Http1xServerRequest.isValidAuthority(Http1xServerRequest.j

### Version

5.0

### Context

When running job https://github.com/eclipse-vertx/vert.x/actions/runs/21745456833/job/62730103189 the following test failed:

```
Error:  Failures: 
Error:    TimerTest.access$600:33->AsyncTestBase.assertTrue:378
```



### Steps to reproduce

This is a random failure in CI.

Looking at the code, it relies on a monotonic clock, wich might have not worked in that execution on a Windows system. 

https://github.com/eclipse-vertx/vert.x/blob/f1a7da10dc12e871b1e2d34a49ea6e3d45f68b0f/vertx-core/src/test/java/io/vertx/tests/timer/TimerTest.java#L126

It would be more reliable to use System.nanoTime() which is guaranteed to be monotonic as a first attempt.


### Do you have a reproducer?

_No response_

### Version

5.0

### Context

When running CI with https://github.com/eclipse-vertx/vert.x/actions/runs/21746150258 I found that the following test failed randomly: 

```
Error:  Errors: 
Error:    VirtualThreadDeploymentTest.testHttpClientStopRequestInProgress:206->AsyncTestBase.assertWaitUntil:659->AsyncTestBase.assertWaitUntil:672 » IllegalState Timed out
```

### Steps to reproduce

This is an unstable CI test. 

Looking at the code, it might be due to not waiting for the server to start up. 

### Do you have a reproducer?

_No response_

input.pr_title: Migration of existing tests to stateless mode
input.pr_body:
- **Update HttpClientConnectionTest and its sub tests to use VertxBase static asserts.**
- **Update HttpMetricsTestBase and its sub tests to use VertxBase static asserts.**
- **Update HttpClientFileUploadTest and its sub tests to use VertxBase static asserts.**
- **Update AsyncMapTest and its sub tests to use VertxBase static asserts.**
- **Update Http3ClientTest/Http3ContextTest/Http3FlowControlTest/Http3ServerTest to use VertxBase static asserts.**
- **Update HttpServerFileUploadTest to use VertxBase static asserts.**
- **Update HttpTracingTestBase to use VertxBase static asserts.**
- **Update HttpSendFileTest to use VertxBase static asserts.**
- **Update Http2ClientTest/Http2ServerTest/HttpBandwidthLimitingTest/Http2MYRServerTest to use VertxBase static asserts.**
- **Update HttpAlternativesTest to use VertxBase static asserts.**
- **Update ConnectionPoolTest to use VertxBase static asserts.**
- **Update SharedServersConcurrencyTest to use VertxBase static asserts.**
- **Update QLogTest/QuicApplicationTest/QuicClientTest/QuicContextTest/QuicFlowControlTest/QuicMetricsTest/QuicServerLoadBalancingTest/TokenManagerTest to use VertxBase static asserts.**
- **Update VertxTest to use VertxBase static asserts.**

Motivation:

The new stateless `VertxTestBase` mode allows to spot incorrect assertions, when a test is migrated we would like to ensure `AsyncTestBase` assertions are never called anymore.

This is useful to prevent regression until the effort is completed.

Changes:

Turn the boolean config into an enum with a `FORBIDDEN` mode always fail on thread checks.

input.pr_title: Migration of tests to VertxTestBase stateless mode
input.pr_body:
Motivation:

Migrate existing usage of `VertxTestBase` to stateless assertions.

An usage is typically migrated with:

1. Switch the test to stateless mode
2. Run the test, it will log incorrect usage of `AsyncTestBase` assertions which cannot reliably fail the test
3. Fix the incorrect unreliable assertions, make sure that those assertions are called from a thread capable of doing so
4. Migrate the test from AsyncTestBase assertion usage and instead static asserts such as JUnit or AssertJ (this can be achieved by an agent)

This covers a portion of the (large) vertx test base.
